Buy My Car · Site Guide 01 / 26

Site guide

How to run your
website & console

A plain walkthrough of every part of your Buy My Car site: the pages your customers use, and the tools you run it all from. One screen at a time.

20 features · Public site & dealer console · Prepared by Jewell Projects

Scroll, or use your arrow keys  ↓

Two halves

Everything lives in two places

Your website

What your customers see and use.

  • Home & reviews
  • Cars for sale
  • Each car’s page
  • Sell your car
  • Finance
  • Compare & saved
  • About, contact, FAQ

Dealer console

Where you run the business, at /admin.

  • Dashboard
  • Adam AI
  • Submissions
  • Inventory
  • Enquiries & finance
  • Site copy
  • Analytics

Part one

Your website

The eleven pages your customers move through, from first look to “I’ll take it”.

Your website · 04

The home page

Your front door. The hero video sets the tone, then the page runs down to your latest cars and your reviews.

  1. 1The big headline and buttons pull people straight to “Browse cars” or “Sell your car”.
  2. 2The quick search jumps a buyer to a make or body type in one tap.
  3. 3Scroll on for latest arrivals, why people deal with you, and the reviews wall.

TipEvery word here is yours to change in Site copy — no developer needed.

Your website · 05

The reviews wall

Proof, at the bottom of the home page. One review fills the screen at a time, like a slideshow.

  1. 1The big number up top is your Google star rating.
  2. 2Reviews rotate on their own; hover to pause.
  3. 3Tap any reviewer’s face to jump straight to what they said.

TipRight now these are sample reviews. Connect the live Google feed and real photos drop straight in.

Your website · 06

Cars for sale

Your whole lot in one place, and the page buyers spend the most time on.

  1. 1Filter by make, price or body type; sort to taste.
  2. 2Each card shows the price and the specs that matter at a glance.
  3. 3Click any car to open its full page.

TipThe watchlist lets a buyer get a message the moment a matching car lands.

Your website · 07

A car’s own page

The full story on one car. Where a browser turns into a buyer.

  1. 1Photo gallery up top, full specs below.
  2. 2“Adam’s take” is your honest word on the car, in your voice.
  3. 3PPSR status shows it’s clear; the buttons let them enquire or book a look.

Your website · 08

Sell your car

How someone sells their car to you, in about five minutes on their phone.

  1. 1They enter the rego and a few details, add photos, and leave their contact.
  2. 2Hitting submit drops it straight into your Submissions.
  3. 3They get a private link to track your offer.

TipNothing to chase — every sell request is waiting for you in the console.

Your website · 09

Finance

Explains how finance works with you, and catches the buyers who want it.

  1. 1Plain-English rundown of the process, no jargon.
  2. 2The buyer leaves their details in the form.
  3. 3It lands in your Finance tab in the console.

Your website · 10

Compare cars

Line up to three cars side by side so a buyer can decide with confidence.

  1. 1Tap the scales icon on any car to add it here.
  2. 2Open Compare to see price, specs and weekly repayment in one table.
  3. 3The best price and lowest kays are highlighted for them.

TipShown here empty. It fills the moment a buyer adds cars.

Your website · 11

Saved cars

A buyer’s shortlist, built with the heart icon on any car.

  1. 1Tap the heart to save a car.
  2. 2Everything saved lives on the Saved page.
  3. 3It stays on their device — no account needed.

Your website · 12

About Adam

Your story. The page that turns a stranger into someone who trusts you.

  1. 1Who you are and how you work, in your own words.
  2. 2Reachable from the top menu on every page.

Your website · 13

Contact

Your phone, hours and location, plus a message form.

  1. 1Buyers can call, or send a message that lands in Enquiries.
  2. 2Opening hours and address come straight from Site copy.

Your website · 14

FAQ

The common questions, answered up front — so you field fewer of the same calls.

  1. 1Grouped by topic and easy to scan.
  2. 2Edit any answer yourself in Site copy.

Part two

Dealer console

The eight tools you run the whole business from, all behind one login.

Dealer console · 16

Logging in

The private door to your console. Only accounts you’ve allowed can get through.

  1. 1Go to your site address followed by /admin.
  2. 2Enter your email and password.
  3. 3There are no public sign-ups — you add who gets in.

TipChange the demo password before you go live.

Dealer console · 17

The dashboard

Your morning glance at the state of the business, in four numbers.

  1. 1New submissions, open enquiries, live listings and sold-this-month, up top.
  2. 2The activity feed shows what’s happened most recently.
  3. 3“View site” jumps you back to the public site any time.

Dealer console · 18

Adam AI

Your own AI offsider. Ask it anything about the business, or get it to write for you.

  1. 1Type a question — it already knows your live stock and submissions.
  2. 2Ask for social captions, a reply to a tricky buyer, or pricing thoughts.
  3. 3Past chats are saved in the side rail; “New chat” starts fresh.

TipIt only knows your business — keep it as your day-to-day sounding board.

Dealer console · 19

Submissions

Every car people want to sell you, in one queue.

  1. 1Open a submission to see the details and photos.
  2. 2Make your offer; the seller is updated automatically.
  3. 3Move it through the stages as the deal progresses.

Dealer console · 20

Inventory

Your live stock — add, edit, publish and mark sold.

  1. 1Add a car with photos, specs and price.
  2. 2Publish to put it on the site; unpublish to pull it.
  3. 3Mark it sold when it’s gone and it updates the site.

Dealer console · 21

Enquiries

Buyer questions and book-a-look requests, all together.

  1. 1See who’s asking and which car they mean.
  2. 2Reply and mark it handled so nothing slips.

Dealer console · 22

Finance leads

Everyone who asked about finance, in one list to work through.

  1. 1Each lead shows what the buyer’s after.
  2. 2Work them top to bottom.

Dealer console · 23

Site copy

Change the words on your site yourself. This is the one that saves you the most.

  1. 1Edit headlines, hero text, reviews, contact details and hours.
  2. 2Hit save and it’s live on the site straight away.
  3. 3No developer, no waiting.

TipThis is where you set your real phone, address, hours and licence number.

Dealer console · 24

Analytics

A read on what’s happening — what people look at and what they act on.

  1. 1See views and which cars draw the most interest.
  2. 2Track enquiries so you know what’s working.

A day on Buy My Car

Five minutes, top to bottom

  1. 1
    Glance at the Dashboard. New submissions, enquiries, what sold.
  2. 2
    Work your Submissions. Review, make offers, keep sellers moving.
  3. 3
    Clear your Enquiries. Reply to buyers, mark them handled.
  4. 4
    Keep Inventory fresh. Add new stock, mark sold cars sold.
  5. 5
    Ask Adam AI. Captions, replies, a second opinion on price.

Before you go live

A short checklist

  • Put your real phone, address, hours and licence number in Site copy.
  • Change the demo login password.
  • Connect the live Google reviews feed.
  • Point the site at your real web address.
  • Have the legal pages checked over.

Anything unclear? Jewell Projects is a message away.
